key_items: Public companies added 43,557 BTC in May as institutional adoption accelerates despite price weakness
Bitcoin ETF outflows of $2.1B in June signal exhausted selling pressure with moderated pace
Bitcoin touches critical support trendline never broken in market cycles, historically signaling reversal opportunity
reasoning: Mixed macro signals show institutional accumulation and technical support holding despite near-term weakness, but substantial ETF outflows and on-chain data indicating continued selling pressure suggest the bottom may not be firmly in place yet. Regulatory tailwinds (Japan ETF bill, Hungary reversal) and traditional finance inflows provide medium-term support, but the 1-7 day swing window faces near-term downside risk from unresolved selling momentum.
risks: On-chain PnL Index still in transition phase and Bitcoin demand falling significantly suggest a lower bottom could form before upside reversal, potentially testing $55,000-$58,000 support within the swing timeframe.
